profile.behavior: Gregarious, often foraging in flocks; feeds chiefly on seeds, buds, berries, and small fruits, crushing seeds with rapid bites. Flight is bouncy and undulating.
field_marks: Small, slender-bodied finch with a fairly large, stout conical bill.
Adult male typically rosy red on the forehead, eyebrow, throat, and upper breast; the intensity can vary toward orange or yellow.
Brown, streaked back, wings, flanks, and tail; females and immatures are grayish-brown with diffuse streaking and no red.
Short, rounded wings make the relatively long, shallowly notched tail conspicuous.
Dark eye and fine dark legs; bill is pale gray to horn-colored with a curved upper profile.
profile.habitat: Semi-open habitats including woodland edges, shrublands, grasslands, dry brushy country, farms, and human-altered areas with trees, weeds, and buildings; generally avoids unbroken forest and grassland.

sources.title: House Finch Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology

findings: Independent verification supports House Finch (Haemorhous mexicanus), family Fringillidae, and the printed length, weight, and wingspan values.

The depicted adult male correctly shows a stout, curved-profile conical bill; dark eye; fine dark legs and feet; rosy-red forehead, eyebrow, throat, and upper breast; and streaky brown back, wings, flanks, and tail. The female/immature head study is appropriately gray-brown and unstreaked red.

The illustrated bird has one head, one beak, two wings (one is occluded by the near wing), two legs, and one long, shallowly notched tail. Its bill is slightly stylized/oversized but remains within an acceptable House Finch depiction.

All visible factual text is consistent with the verified identification, measurements, field marks, behavior, diet, and habitat. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: House Finch Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
